strengths: Strong SQL and DB query optimization
Robust data visualization and reporting skills
Quantified impact on revenue and efficiency
weaknesses: Limited explicit supply chain domain experience
AWS experience limited to S3 only
ETL DW concepts not explicitly detailed
Short total years in formal analytics roles
improvement_bullets: Developed robust ETL DW concepts by architecting end-to-end data pipelines in Snowflake and BigQuery, ensuring high data availability for downstream supply chain reporting.
Led data migration efforts, transitioning legacy manual reporting into automated cloud-based environments to support scalable analytics and AI-driven decision-making.
Leveraged AWS S3 as a primary data lake integration point to streamline the ingestion of diverse datasets for enterprise-level supply chain analysis.
Built interactive Tableau dashboards to provide self-service analytics, utilizing advanced data visualization techniques to track supply chain KPIs and business trends.
Collaborated with cross-functional supply chain teams to align data requirements with business objectives, identifying operational inefficiencies through deep-dive analysis of complex datasets.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Business Analyst, Worldwide Grocery Stores (WWGS) opportunity with Amazon.com. I am interested in this opportunity because of Amazon’s commitment to leveraging advanced analytics to optimize complex supply chain ecosystems and deliver innovation at scale. What interests me about working for Amazon is its customer-obsessed approach to data, which resonates with my background in transforming raw technical data into strategic business value. I believe my expertise in ETL data pipeline architecture and my proficiency in Tableau for business intelligence would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my career, I have approached complex data problems by bridging the gap between technical infrastructure and operational strategy. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I identified a need for standardized KPIs and automated reporting; I built Python-based ETL workflows that replaced manual processes, resulting in a 40% improvement in operational efficiency and a 300% revenue increase in targeted segments. Ultimately, I know that the Business Analyst, Worldwide Grocery Stores (WWGS) role at Amazon.com would allow me to bring my data modeling skills to your supply chain analytical initiatives. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead enabled me to develop my stakeholder collaboration skills. At Shine Masters, I acted as the primary liaison between technical systems and executive leadership, translating complex statistical models into actionable recommendations that informed marketing and operational spend. This experience in translating technical complexity into clear business narratives is exactly what I plan to bring to your team. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my SQL and data visualization skills in working on the integration of core datasets into AI platforms at Amazon.com. I know that my technical proficiency in cloud data platforms and my analytical mindset align with what it takes to succeed in this role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
